summary: README/report wording now matches the canonical non-phase ylong_base 200/1000/3000 fair matrix after drain-buffer reuse, states that the current evidence refutes SOTA thresholds rather than proving a performance claim, and separately records the tokio_base candidate matrix as a stronger but still non-SOTA counterexample.

claims_revised: Benchmark description separates curl_cli and libcurl.
Checked-in setup is documented as non-phase 200/1000/3000 requests, five repeats, warmup 50, empty request body, and 4096-byte response body.
Result table reports the current nine scenario/request-count rows.
README explicitly says the full fair matrix is not a SOTA performance claim and records the 0.99x geomean / 0.77x worst-cell counterexample.
README separately records the tokio_base candidate matrix at about 1.07x geomean / 0.86x worst cell and keeps it out of the canonical table.
Parser and plot labels distinguish curl_cli from libcurl.
Driver exposes --baseline curl-cli|libcurl|both so reruns declare the baseline model.
Driver exposes --scenario including all, HTTPS origin, and proxy-mTLS HTTPS-origin topologies.
Checked-in summary now contains scenario, repeat_count, confidence half-width columns, and libcurl fair-matrix metrics.
Checked-in comparison now contains per-scenario ylong/libcurl ratios for elapsed, latency, p50, p95, CPU, throughput, and RSS.
Regenerated figure uses libcurl baseline labeling for the checked-in fair matrix.

diff_checks.observed: README benchmark section separates curl_cli and libcurl, documents --scenario all, reports all nine scenario/request-count rows, and scopes checked-in results as fair-matrix counterexample evidence.

diff_checks.command: rg -n "Checked-in fair-matrix|SOTA performance claim|0\.99x|0\.77x|connection-reuse trace" README.md
diff_checks.observed: README identifies the fair matrix, current non-SOTA result, and reuse-trace boundary.
README identifies the tokio_base candidate matrix as stronger than canonical ylong_base but still non-SOTA.

supported_claims_after_revision: HTTPS proxy support includes proxy TLS and proxy mTLS endpoint tests.
CONNECT parser accepts complete 2xx responses and preserves fail-closed error handling.
sync and async share proxy semantics, not one connector implementation.
Benchmark harness can execute a same-process libcurl library baseline.
Benchmark harness can execute HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy, HTTPS-origin-over-HTTPS-proxy, and proxy-mTLS HTTPS-origin scenarios.
Checked-in performance output is fair-matrix evidence that currently refutes SOTA threshold completion.
tokio_base candidate output is recorded separately as candidate counterexample evidence, not a checked-in canonical speedup claim.
intentionally_not_claimed: No final SOTA performance status is claimed from the fair matrix.
No curl CLI process result is presented as libcurl-library evidence.
No micro-optimization is presented as the causal source of performance advantage.
The tokio_base candidate matrix is not presented as SOTA completion evidence.
claim_boundaries: HTTPS proxy support includes proxy TLS and proxy mTLS endpoint tests.
Benchmark harness can execute a same-process libcurl library baseline.
Benchmark harness records server-side connection reuse traces and keeps phase diagnostics separate from threshold outputs.
Checked-in performance output is fair-matrix evidence and currently refutes, rather than proves, SOTA performance.
The tokio_base candidate matrix is tracked separately because it improves the point estimate but still fails hard SOTA gates.
No final SOTA performance status is claimed.

verification_commands.observed: README identifies the canonical fair matrix, tokio_base candidate, current non-SOTA results, and reuse-trace boundary.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_s9_reuse_check CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo check -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0,libcurl_bench

PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_proxy_tests CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_async_https_proxy_tls --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,tokio_base,c_openssl_3_0

PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_proxy_tests CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_sync_https_proxy_tls --no-default-features --features sync,async,http1_1,tokio_base,c_openssl_3_0
verification_commands.observed: 2 tests passed. The sync-only tokio_base feature combination is not valid in this crate because runtime Semaphore/Sleep exports are gated by async; adding async is the valid test build.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_proxy_no_tls CARGO_NET_GIT_FETCH_WITH_CLI=true GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0='url.https://github.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git.insteadOf' GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0='https://gitee.com/openharmony/commonlibrary_rust_ylong_runtime.git' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_https_proxy_no_tls --no-default-features --features async,sync,http1_1,tokio_base

verification_commands.observed: Only nightly-only rustfmt.toml option warnings were emitted.
goal_achieved_permitted=false; S9, S10, final-report.json, and runtime counterexample review remain incomplete.
